pi_items.source: setup_task.sh creates the dungeon design specification file at this exact path using a heredoc cat command
export_result.sh references this file path; task description specifies saving diagram as sunken_crypt.drawio
export_result.sh references this file path; task description specifies exporting PNG as sunken_crypt.png
setup_task.sh creates design spec with room header '1. ENTRY HALL (Start)'; metadata claims first room is 'Entry' which is incomplete
Exact room names extracted from ROOM LIST & LAYOUT section in dungeon_design_spec.txt generated by setup_task.sh. First room name is materially different (Entry vs ENTRY HALL).
All 5 items verified in design spec: Start in Entry Hall, Rusty Key in Armory, Trap in Shrine, Loot in Treasure Vault, Boss in Boss Chamber

Room connection analysis from design spec: (1) ENTRY HALL ↔ GRAND HALL, (2) GRAND HALL ↔ ARMORY, (3) GRAND HALL ↔ SHRINE, (4) GRAND HALL ↔ BOSS CHAMBER, (5) SHRINE ↔ TREASURE VAULT. Total = 5 unique undirected edges. Metadata incorrectly claims 6.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires designing a dungeon layout diagram for 'The Sunken Crypt' in draw.io. The dungeon specification file is created at /home/ga/Desktop/dungeon_design_spec.txt and defines six rooms: ENTRY HALL, GRAND HALL, ARMORY, SHRINE, TREASURE VAULT, and BOSS CHAMBER. The specification requires five labeled items: Start, Rusty Key, Trap, Loot, and Boss. The dungeon has five room connections: ENTRY HALL-GRAND HALL, GRAND HALL-ARMORY, GRAND HALL-SHRINE, GRAND HALL-BOSS CHAMBER, and SHRINE-TREASURE VAULT. The agent must export the diagram as sunken_crypt.drawio and sunken_crypt.png to /home/ga/Desktop/. This is a synthetic task with all content programmatically generated via setup_task.sh.
